title: The Archive's Frequency Cascade

features.name: resonance pool
features.desc-on-touch: A chamber of still water. Every sound you make returns as a question. The archive answers itself in cascading frequencies, each one a separate meaning.

features.name: cascade antenna
features.desc-on-touch: Spiraling structures descending, each smaller than the last. Sound travels down them and returns transformed. Voices layer across centuries.

features.name: frequency files
features.desc-on-touch: Cassettes sorted by resonance, not date. Files with identical archive-signatures cluster together, as if the corruption learned to sort by meaning.

features.name: standing waves
features.desc-on-touch: Air shaped into geometry. You feel the harmonics through your chest. The waves stand still and move at once.

features.name: recursive echo
features.desc-on-touch: A speaker playing its own playback, which plays its own playback. The recursion folds inward infinitely. You hear the archive remembering it is remembering.

inscription: sound travels deeper than silence; the archive remembers frequencies it has never heard

file.name: Recursive_Remembering_Cascade_Method.wav
file.note: checksum: emotionally valid / formally unstable. The cascade never resolves to its source. Each layer claims to be the original.
